steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: In a field where creativity is prized, readers who see the novel as merely fictionalized true events would underestimate its creative achievement. The question asks what consequence logically follows.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — *Journey to the End of the Night* is less of an imaginative achievement than it actually is.: The work would appear less imaginatively accomplished than it actually is—a direct consequence of readers discounting the creative invention involved. ✓
  • Choice B — the real-world counterparts of other characters in *Journey to the End of the Night* are hard to identify.: Focuses on identifying real-life counterparts, which the passage does not address as a concern. ✗
  • Choice C — Céline should not have claimed that *Journey to the End of the Night* is based on real events.: Prescribes decisions the author should make, which goes beyond what the passage discusses. ✗
  • Choice D — critics disagree about whether *Journey to the End of the Night* shows greater originality than works without autobiographical elements.: Introduces critic disagreement that is never mentioned in the passage. ✗
That matches Choice A — *Journey to the End of the Night* is less of an imaginative achievement than it actually is..
